key_strengths: 72% global foundry market share and 90%+ at leading-edge nodes with no viable alternative
Exceptional economics: 49% ROIC, 60% gross margins, $31B free cash flow in 2025
Fortress balance sheet: $95B cash, $31B debt, 0.34x debt-to-EBITDA, AA-/Aa3 ratings
Decade-wide moat through mutually reinforcing technology leadership, switching costs, and ecosystem lock-in
Disciplined management: near-zero dilution, no empire-building M&A, 28% dividend increase
key_risks: Geopolitical concentration: 90%+ of advanced chip production on Taiwan, 100 miles from China — existential, unhedgeable tail risk
Valuation demands near-perfect execution: 34x trailing P/E (53% above 10-year average), base-case DCF ~$290 vs ~$339 market price
AI spending concentration: HPC grew to 58% of revenue; NVIDIA alone is 19% — cyclical demand cliff risk if hyperscaler capex slows
Record capex ($52-56B in 2026, $165B Arizona commitment) creates overcapacity risk if demand disappoints
Overseas fabs cost 30-50% more than Taiwan equivalents, creating structural margin pressure as geographic diversification grows
red_flags: Warren Buffett sold entire TSM position within six months specifically due to geopolitical risk
Upside-to-downside asymmetry approximately 1:1 (bull ~$450-500, bear ~$160-260) — far short of 2-3x required
Minimal insider ownership means management personal wealth not deeply at risk
Japan fab losses tripled to NT$9.8B in 2025
buy_triggers: Forward P/E compresses below 20x (~$260/ADR) on market-wide or sector pullback while business thesis remains intact
Meaningful geopolitical de-escalation across the Taiwan Strait (diplomatic breakthrough, sustained reduction in military activity)
Arizona 2nm fabs reach yields and cost parity within 20% of Taiwan operations
sell_triggers: Taiwan Strait crisis moves from theoretical to operational — military mobilization, naval blockade, or sustained disruption to shipping lanes
Samsung or Intel closes yield gap at leading-edge nodes to within 10 percentage points of TSMC
AI capex spending by hyperscalers declines for two consecutive quarters while TSMC is mid-ramp on record capacity expansion
valuation_summary: Base-case DCF yields approximately $290/ADR versus a ~$339 market price, offering no margin of safety; at 34x trailing earnings (53% above the 10-year average of 22x), the stock requires roughly 20% annual earnings growth through 2030 just to deliver a 10% annualized return.
source_summary: Analysis based on web searches conducted 2026-03-07 covering TSMC Q4 2025 earnings, FY2025 financials, foundry market share data, technology roadmap updates, and geopolitical risk analyses from TSMC investor relations, Investing.com, CNBC, TrendForce, and other sources.
